revision.text:
== Overview == Citrix XenApp and XenDesktop environment providing virtual desktops and published applications to end users.
== Citrix Servers == Current production Citrix servers: * CTX-01 - Delivery Controller (Primary) * CTX-02 - Delivery Controller (Secondary) * CTX-03 - StoreFront Server * CTX-04 - NetScaler Gateway * CTX-W01 - Worker / VDA Node 1 * CTX-W02 - Worker / VDA Node 2 * CTX-W03 - Worker / VDA Node 3
== Licensing == Citrix license server: LIC-01.internal License type: XenApp Platinum Total licenses: 500 concurrent
== Starting and Stopping Services == To restart the Citrix Broker Service on a Delivery Controller: net stop CitrixBrokerService net start CitrixBrokerService
To restart StoreFront: iisreset /restart
== Common Issues ==
=== Users Cannot Log In === # Check Delivery Controller service status on CTX-01 and CTX-02 # Check StoreFront availability at https://storefront.internal # Review event logs on the affected Delivery Controller # Verify NetScaler Gateway is reachable
=== Sessions Stuck or Disconnected === Use Citrix Studio or run the following PowerShell on CTX-01: Get-BrokerSession | Where-Object {$_.SessionState -eq "Disconnected"} | Stop-BrokerSession
=== High CPU on Worker Nodes === # RDP to the affected VDA node # Use Task Manager to identify the process # Check Citrix Director for active session count # Escalate to Citrix admin if load exceeds 85%
== Monitoring == Citrix environment is monitored via: * Citrix Director: https://ctx-01.internal/Director * SCOM alerts configured for broker service failures * Nagios checks on port 80/443 for StoreFront
== Contacts == * Citrix Admin: citrix-team@company.com * Vendor Support: 1-800-CITRIX (contract #CTX-2023-4451)

== Overview == This page documents all physical and virtual servers in the production environment.
== Physical Servers ==
=== Data Center A === {| class="wikitable" ! Hostname !! Role !! OS !! CPU !! RAM !! Location |- | SRV-DC01 || Domain Controller (Primary) || Windows Server 2019 || 8 vCPU || 32GB || Rack A1 |- | SRV-DC02 || Domain Controller (Secondary) || Windows Server 2019 || 8 vCPU || 32GB || Rack A1 |- | SRV-FILE01 || File Server || Windows Server 2022 || 16 vCPU || 64GB || Rack A2 |- | SRV-SQL01 || SQL Server (Primary) || Windows Server 2019 + SQL 2019 || 32 vCPU || 256GB || Rack B1 |- | SRV-SQL02 || SQL Server (Secondary/Mirror) || Windows Server 2019 + SQL 2019 || 32 vCPU || 256GB || Rack B2 |}
=== Data Center B (DR) === * SRV-DC03 - Domain Controller (DR) * SRV-FILE02 - File Server (DR, DFS Replica) * SRV-SQL03 - SQL Server (DR, Log Shipping target)
== Virtual Machines (VMware) == vCenter: vcenter.internal Cluster: PROD-CLUSTER-01
Key VMs: * APP-WEB01 / APP-WEB02 - Web application servers (IIS) * APP-API01 / APP-API02 - API servers * MON-01 - Monitoring server (Nagios/SCOM) * BACKUP-01 - Veeam backup server
== Server Patching == Patching schedule: * Production servers: 3rd Sunday of each month, 02:00-06:00 * DR servers: Week after production * Emergency patches: Change request required, CAB approval
Patching procedure: # Create change request in ServiceNow # Take VM snapshot (virtual servers) # Apply patches via WSUS or manual # Verify services are running post-patch # Remove snapshot after 72 hours if stable
== Backup Policy == * Veeam Backup & Replication manages all VM backups * Retention: 14 daily, 4 weekly, 12 monthly * Backup window: 22:00-05:00 daily * Backup target: SAN storage + offsite tape (monthly) * RTO: 4 hours | RPO: 24 hours
== Hardware Refresh == Next scheduled hardware refresh: Q2 2026

== Overview == The monitoring stack consists of Nagios Core for infrastructure checks and SCOM for Windows-specific monitoring.
== Monitoring Tools == * '''Nagios''': https://mon-01.internal/nagios — Linux/network/service checks * '''SCOM 2019''': https://scom.internal — Windows server monitoring * '''Grafana''': https://grafana.internal — Performance dashboards * '''PagerDuty''': https://company.pagerduty.com — Alert routing and on-call
== Nagios Checks == Critical checks currently configured: * Ping / host availability — all servers * Disk usage threshold: Warning 80%, Critical 90% * CPU threshold: Warning 85%, Critical 95% * Windows services: key services per server role * SQL Server availability and replication lag * Website availability (HTTP 200 checks)
== Alert Severity Levels == {| class="wikitable" ! Level !! Response Time !! Examples |- | P1 - Critical || Immediate (24/7) || Server down, database unavailable, widespread outage |- | P2 - High || 30 minutes (business hours), 1 hour (out of hours) || Single service failure, performance degradation |- | P3 - Medium || 4 hours (business hours) || Non-critical service warning, disk space warning |- | P4 - Low || Next business day || Informational alerts, capacity planning |}
== Escalation Procedure == # Alert fires in PagerDuty → On-call engineer notified # On-call acknowledges within SLA or escalates to secondary # If unresolved after 30 minutes → escalate to team lead # P1 incidents → also notify: IT Manager, relevant service owner
== On-Call Rota == On-call schedule managed in PagerDuty. Primary on-call rotation: weekly, Monday 09:00 handover Secondary escalation: senior engineers
== Adding a New Check == # SSH to mon-01.internal # Edit /etc/nagios/conf.d/hosts.cfg or relevant service file # Run: nagios -v /etc/nagios/nagios.cfg # Reload: systemctl reload nagios # Verify check appears in web UI
== Known Issues == * SCOM agent on SRV-FILE01 reports false positives on disk — suppressed until storage team resolves (ticket #INC-4421) * Grafana dashboard for SQL replication is outdated — rebuild planned for Q1 2026

== Overview == This document defines the standard incident response procedure for all IT infrastructure incidents.
== Incident Classification ==
=== P1 - Critical === * Complete service outage affecting all users * Data loss or data breach * Security incident
=== P2 - High === * Partial service outage (significant user impact) * Performance degradation affecting business operations
=== P3 - Medium === * Single user or small group impacted * Non-critical system failure
== Response Steps ==
=== P1 Incident === # '''Declare''' the incident — create ticket in ServiceNow, set priority P1 # '''Notify''' — page on-call via PagerDuty, notify IT Manager immediately # '''Assemble''' bridge call — Teams/Zoom bridge: https://company.zoom.us/j/incident # '''Investigate''' — assign investigation lead, document findings in ticket # '''Communicate''' — send status update to stakeholders every 30 minutes # '''Resolve''' — apply fix, verify services restored # '''Post-incident''' — PIR (Post Incident Review) within 48 hours
=== P2 Incident === # Create ServiceNow ticket # Notify team lead # Investigate and resolve within SLA # Update ticket with resolution notes
== Communication Templates ==
=== Initial Notification === Subject: [P1 INCIDENT] <Service> - <Brief Description>
We are currently investigating an issue with <service>. Impact: <who is affected> Started: <time> Next update: 30 minutes
=== Resolution Notice === Subject: [RESOLVED] [P1 INCIDENT] <Service> - <Brief Description>
The incident has been resolved as of <time>. Root cause: <brief description> Action taken: <what was done> PIR scheduled: <date>
== Post Incident Review (PIR) == PIR template is in SharePoint: IT Team > Incidents > PIR Template.docx
Required sections: * Timeline of events * Root cause analysis * Impact assessment * Actions to prevent recurrence * Owner and due dates for each action
== Contacts == * IT Manager: it-manager@company.com / ext 5001 * Security Team: security@company.com (P1 security incidents only) * Network Team: network-ops@company.com

== Overview == Remote access to internal resources is provided via Cisco AnyConnect VPN and Citrix Gateway.
== VPN Infrastructure == * Primary VPN: Cisco ASA 5525-X (vpn.company.com) * Backup VPN: Cisco ASA 5515-X (vpn2.company.com) * Authentication: Active Directory + Duo MFA
== Connecting via VPN == # Download Cisco AnyConnect from the IT portal: https://itportal.internal/vpn # Install and launch AnyConnect # Enter server: vpn.company.com # Authenticate with your AD username and password # Approve the Duo MFA push notification # Connected — you can now access internal resources
== VPN Groups == {| class="wikitable" ! Group !! Access Level !! Who Uses It |- | STAFF-VPN || Standard internal network access || All staff |- | ADMIN-VPN || Full network access including server subnet || IT staff only |- | VENDOR-VPN || Limited access to specific systems || Third-party vendors |}
== Troubleshooting VPN ==
=== Cannot Connect === * Verify credentials are correct (test at https://owa.company.com) * Check Duo is working — try logging into https://duo.company.com * Try the backup VPN: vpn2.company.com * If all fail, contact helpdesk
=== Slow VPN Performance === * Use split tunnelling profile if available * Connect from a wired connection where possible * Check VPN gateway load at: https://mon-01.internal/nagios (VPN checks)
== Citrix Remote Access == For users who only need application access (not full VPN): * URL: https://storefront.internal or https://citrix.company.com (external) * No VPN client required * Uses same AD credentials + Duo MFA
